title: Software Engineer II, Backend (ML Training & Serving)

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Software Engineer with 4+ years building scalable backend systems and cloud-native applications using Python, Java, REST APIs, MySQL, AWS, and Kubernetes. Delivered Machine Learning-integrated services and event-driven systems with 99.99% reliability, improving performance by 35% while strengthening cross-team communication and engineering operations across distributed code bases.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Architected scalable backend services in Python and Java Spring Boot microservices, enabling modular service-oriented architecture and independent deployments across the engineering code base.
Engineered Python-based data processing pipelines and automation scripts for backend operations, reducing manual operational effort by ~40% and improving processing efficiency.
Designed Machine Learning-powered backend services integrating Python LLM APIs for intelligent text processing, classification, and automated workflows.
Optimized complex SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models across MySQL and NoSQL databases, improving reporting and transaction performance by 35%.
Built event-driven systems using DynamoDB Streams, Python/Node.js Lambda functions, and SNS/SQS, ensuring 99.99% reliability at high throughput.
Developed RESTful and GraphQL APIs consumed by Angular frontends, ensuring seamless integration and clear documentation through strong technical communication.
Deployed cloud-native applications on AWS (Lambda, ECS, EC2, S3, RDS, DynamoDB) with Kubernetes orchestration, cutting production issue resolution time by 40% via Splunk monitoring.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed backend applications in Python, Java Spring Boot, and Node.js, delivering scalable RESTful APIs and microservices serving high-volume traffic.
Built Python automation scripts for data transformation, validation, and backend task orchestration, streamlining operations across teams.
Optimized SQL queries, joins, views, and stored procedures on MySQL, improving API response times by 35%.
Created and documented REST APIs using Swagger/OpenAPI, enforcing API versioning and backward compatibility through clear written communication.
Implemented event-driven architecture with Apache Kafka, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35%.
Containerized applications with Docker and Kubernetes and provisioned AWS and GCP infrastructure via Terraform, improving CI/CD deployment efficiency.
Applied Redis caching, OAuth2/JWT security, and SQL optimization to improve throughput under heavy load.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular and Python/Java backend services during a structured engineering training program.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational schemas for transactional systems, working extensively with MySQL and MongoDB to cut query execution time by 20%.
Developed RESTful APIs and documented service contracts using Swagger/OpenAPI, sharpening writing and communication skills.
Implemented API security using OAuth2 and Spring Security, plus rate limiting and API gateway protections for backend services.
Increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python-based test automation tools.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University Of North Carolina At Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate - AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Reconstructed mangled experience entries into three clean roles (Vanguard Software Engineer, Xebia Associate Software Engineer, Xebia Engineer Trainee) with proper titles, companies, and dates.
Wrote a Software Engineer summary mirroring the exact target title with years, core skills, and a quantified outcome.
Wove required keywords into bullets: Python, Java/C, Kotlin (skills), REST, MySQL, AWS, Kubernetes, machine learning, operations, training, writing, communication, code base, engineering, systems.
Converted all fragments into strong action-verb bullets (Architected, Engineered, Optimized, Built, Deployed) and removed weak openers.
Merged degree and institution into one education entry; cleaned certification formatting.
Deduplicated and reordered the Skills list to lead with required skills.
Estimated/retained metrics to confirm: ~40% manual effort reduction, 35% performance gains, 99.99% reliability, 40% resolution-time reduction, 20% query improvement, 95% test coverage.
Note: JD contained HTML/markup fragments (data-error, data-placeholder, font-weight, etc.) that are not real skills, so they were intentionally excluded to keep the resume clean and ATS-safe.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
Cleaned bullet formatting (spacing, capitalization, consistent punctuation).
